title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Go Client Systems · Execution Trace: Sensitive Field Forwarding
summary: A webhook verifier encounters cross-host redirects, rewritten methods, and sensitive authorization headers while resolving a vendor migration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go sensitive field forwarding increment in a Go 1.26 net/http auditable redirect policy engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Strip or deliberately reissue authorization, cookies, origin-sensitive fields, and bodies when authority changes. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Bound redirect hops, detect loops, retain the chain, and expose the policy decision to callers. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Sensitive Field Forwarding · Theory Sensitive Field Forwarding
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model sends POST G-6 with authorization, then receives 302 ../moved, 307 https://upload.example.test/next, and a loop back to the first URL. Predict method, body, credential, origin, hop-budget, and SSRF decisions per step.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Sensitive Field Forwarding contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Go Predict Sensitive Field Forwarding · Theory Sensitive Field Forwarding.

For Go Predict Sensitive Field Forwarding · Theory Sensitive Field Forwarding, keep Sensitive Field Forwarding connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
